revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=忍坂坐生根神社| 画像=| 所在地=[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]忍坂871| 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 31 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N| 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 21 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E| 祭神=[[少彦名神]]| 社格=[[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]]| 本殿=なし| 神体=宮山（[[神体山]]）| 創建=不詳| 例祭=[[10月17日]]| 地図=Japan Nara| }} '''忍坂坐生根神社'''（おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]にある神社である。[[式内大社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == *[[少彦名神]] 『[[特選神名牒]]』や[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）の『郷村社取調帳』には「祭神生根神」と記されており、この「生根神」については不詳である。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳だが、[[天平]]2年（[[730年]]）の『[[大和国]]正税帳』に「生根神戸」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年([[806年]])の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』に「生根神一戸大和」と名前が見えることからそれ以前からの創建と見られる。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では大社に列し、月次・新嘗の[[奉幣]]に預ると記されている。[[江戸時代]]には単に「生根神社」と称しており、[[磐座]]もあることから移転することなくこの地で祭祀されていたとされる。かつては[[神宮寺]]として[[元禄]]2年（[[1689年]]）に建立された石井寺薬師堂があった。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下より従五位上（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「忍坂生根神」 == 境内 == 当社に本殿はなく、拝殿の北側に「石神」と称する自然石21個を並べた[[磐座]]を[[神体]]として祀る。 == 交通アクセス == * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[大阪線]][[大和朝倉駅]]から南方へ1km == 関連項目 == * [[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 忍坂坐生根神社] - 桜井市観光協会 {{デフォルトソート:おしさかにますいくねしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{神社| 名称=忍坂坐生根神社| 画像=| 所在地=[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]忍坂871| 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 31 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N| 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 21 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E| 祭神=[[少彦名神]]| 社格=[[式内社]]（大）

{{出典の明記|date=2018-3}} {{神社 | 名称=忍坂坐生根神社 | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=桜井市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | 所在地=[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]忍坂871 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 31 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 21 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 祭神=[[少彦名神]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] | 本殿=なし | 神体=宮山（[[神体山]]） | 創建=不詳 | 例祭=[[10月17日]] | 地図=Japan Nara }} '''忍坂坐生根神社'''（おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]にある神社である。[[式内大社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == *[[少彦名神]] 『[[特選神名牒]]』や[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）の『郷村社取調帳』には「祭神生根神」と記されており、この「生根神」については不詳である。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳だが、[[天平]]2年（[[730年]]）の『[[大和国]]正税帳』に「生根神戸」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』に「生根神一戸大和」と名前が見えることからそれ以前からの創建と見られる。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では大社に列し、月次・新嘗の[[奉幣]]に預ると記されている。[[江戸時代]]には単に「生根神社」と称しており、[[磐座]]もあることから移転することなくこの地で祭祀されていたとされる。かつては[[神宮寺]]として[[元禄]]2年（[[1689年]]）に建立された石井寺薬師堂があった。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下より従五位上（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「忍坂生根神」 == 境内 == 当社に本殿はなく、拝殿の北側に「石神」と称する自然石21個を並べた[[磐座]]を[[神体]]として祀る。 == 交通アクセス == * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[大阪線]][[大和朝倉駅]]から南方へ1km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 忍坂坐生根神社] - 桜井市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:おしさかにますいくねしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine'''|忍坂坐生根神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{出典の明記|date=2018-3}} {{神社 | 名称=忍坂坐生根神社 | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=桜井市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | 所在地=[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]忍坂871 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 31 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 21 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 祭神=[[少彦名神]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] | 本殿=なし | 神体=宮山（[[神体山]]） | 創建=不詳 | 例祭=[[10月17日]] | 地図=Japan Nara }} '''忍坂坐生根神社'''（おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]にある神社である。[[式内大社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == *[[少彦名神]] 『[[特選神名牒]]』や[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）の『郷村社取調帳』には「祭神生根神」と記されており、この「生根神」については不詳である。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳だが、[[天平]]2年（[[730年]]）の『[[大和国]]正税帳』に「生根神戸」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』に「生根神一戸大和」と名前が見えることからそれ以前からの創建と見られる。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では大社に列し、月次・新嘗の[[奉幣]]に預ると記されている。[[江戸時代]]には単に「生根神社」と称しており、[[磐座]]もあることから移転することなくこの地で祭祀されていたとされる。かつては[[神宮寺]]として[[元禄]]2年（[[1689年]]）に建立された石井寺薬師堂があった。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下より従五位上（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「忍坂生根神」 == 境内 == 当社に本殿はなく、拝殿の北側に「石神」と称する自然石21個を並べた[[磐座]]を[[神体]]として祀る。 == 交通アクセス == * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[大阪線]][[大和朝倉駅]]から南方へ1km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 忍坂坐生根神社] - 桜井市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:おしさかにますいくねしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2018-3}} {{神社 | 名称=忍坂坐生根神社 | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=桜井市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | 所在地=[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]忍坂871 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 31 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 21 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 祭神=[[少彦名神]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] | 本殿=なし | 神体=宮山（[[神体山]]） | 創建=不詳 | 例祭=[[10月17日]] | 地図=Japan Nara }} '''忍坂坐生根神社'''（おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]にある神社である。[[式内大社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == *[[少彦名神]] 『[[特選神名牒]]』や[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）の『郷村社取調帳』には「祭神生根神」と記されており、この「生根神」については不詳である。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳だが、[[天平]]2年（[[730年]]）の『[[大和国]]正税帳』に「生根神戸」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』に「生根神一戸大和」と名前が見えることからそれ以前からの創建と見られる。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では大社に列し、月次・新嘗の[[奉幣]]に預ると記されている。[[江戸時代]]には単に「生根神社」と称しており、[[磐座]]もあることから移転することなくこの地で祭祀されていたとされる。かつては[[神宮寺]]として[[元禄]]2年（[[1689年]]）に建立された石井寺薬師堂があった。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下より従五位上（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「忍坂生根神」 == 境内 == 当社に本殿はなく、拝殿の北側に「石神」と称する自然石21個を並べた[[磐座]]を[[神体]]として祀る。 == 交通アクセス == * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[大阪線]][[大和朝倉駅]]から南方へ1km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 忍坂坐生根神社] - 桜井市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|忍坂坐生根神社|version=106919449|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2018-3}} {{神社 | 名称=忍坂坐生根神社 | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=桜井市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | 所在地=[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]忍坂871 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 31 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 21 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 祭神=[[少彦名神]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] | 本殿=なし | 神体=宮山（[[神体山]]） | 創建=不詳 | 例祭=[[10月17日]] | 地図=Japan Nara }} '''忍坂坐生根神社'''（おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]にある神社である。[[式内大社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == *[[少彦名神]] 『[[特選神名牒]]』や[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）の『郷村社取調帳』には「祭神生根神」と記されており、この「生根神」については不詳である。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳だが、[[天平]]2年（[[730年]]）の『[[大和国]]正税帳』に「生根神戸」、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』に「生根神一戸大和」と名前が見えることからそれ以前からの創建と見られる。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では大社に列し、月次・新嘗の[[奉幣]]に預ると記されている。[[江戸時代]]には単に「生根神社」と称しており、[[磐座]]もあることから移転することなくこの地で祭祀されていたとされる。かつては[[神宮寺]]として[[元禄]]2年（[[1689年]]）に建立された石井寺薬師堂があった。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下より従五位上（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「忍坂生根神」 == 境内 == 当社に本殿はなく、拝殿の北側に「石神」と称する自然石21個を並べた[[磐座]]を[[神体]]として祀る。 == 交通アクセス == * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[大阪線]][[大和朝倉駅]]から南方へ1km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大和国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 忍坂坐生根神社] - 桜井市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9150005003875 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県桜井市忍坂871', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:忍坂坐生根神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153274}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2018-3}} {{神社 | 名称=忍坂坐生根神社 | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=桜井市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | 所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|桜井市|Q127026}}忍坂871 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 31 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 21 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 祭神={{jalink|少彦名神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 本殿=なし | 神体=宮山（{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}） | 創建=不詳 | 例祭={{jalink|10月17日|Q2962}} | 地図=Japan Nara }} '''忍坂坐生根神社'''（おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|桜井市|Q127026}}にある神社である。{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|少彦名神}} 『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』や{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}）の『郷村社取調帳』には「祭神生根神」と記されており、この「生根神」については不詳である。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳だが、{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}2年（{{jalink|730年|Q23563}}）の『{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}正税帳』に「生根神戸」、{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）の『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』に「生根神一戸大和」と名前が見えることからそれ以前からの創建と見られる。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では大社に列し、月次・新嘗の{{jalink|奉幣|Q11445508}}に預ると記されている。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には単に「生根神社」と称しており、{{jalink|磐座|Q11587880}}もあることから移転することなくこの地で祭祀されていたとされる。かつては{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}として{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}2年（{{jalink|1689年|Q7674}}）に建立された石井寺薬師堂があった。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下より従五位上（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）- 表記は「忍坂生根神」 === 境内 === 当社に本殿はなく、拝殿の北側に「石神」と称する自然石21個を並べた{{jalink|磐座|Q11587880}}を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}として祀る。 === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085|近鉄}}{{jalink|大阪線}}{{jalink|大和朝倉駅|Q8047967}}から南方へ1km === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} === 外部リンク === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 忍坂坐生根神社] - 桜井市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:おしさかにますいくねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

桜井市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:おしさかにますいくねしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location=Nara Prefecture, Sakurai City, Oshisaka 871 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 31 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 21 | E/W = E | Enshrined deity=Sukunahikona-no-Kami | Shrine rank=Shikinaisha (Taisha)
Former village shrine | Main hall=None | Sacred object=Miyayama (sacred mountain) | Established=Unknown | Annual festival=October 17 | Map=Japan Nara }} '''Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine''' (おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha (Taisha), and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * Sukunahikona-no-Kami In sources such as the *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō* of Meiji 7 (1874), the enshrined deity is recorded as “Ikune-no-Kami.” However, details regarding this “Ikune-no-Kami” are unknown. === History === === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown, but since the name appears as “Ikune Shrine households” in the *Yamato Province Shōzeichō* of Tenpyō 2 (730), and as “Ikune Shrine, one household, Yamato” in the *Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō* of Daidō 1 (806), it is believed to have been founded earlier than these records. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, it is listed as a Taisha and noted as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals. During the Edo period, it was simply called “Ikune Shrine,” and because there are also sacred rock formations (iwakura), it is believed that rituals were conducted at this site without relocation. In the past, there was also a shrine-temple (jingūji), the Ishii-dera Yakushi Hall, built in Genroku 2 (1689). === Divine rank === * January 27, Ten’an 3 (859): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (as recorded in the *Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) — recorded under the name “Oshisaka Ikune Shrine.” === Precincts === There is no main hall at this shrine. To the north of the worship hall, 21 natural stones known as “Ishigami” are arranged and venerated as the sacred object in the form of an iwakura. === Access === * Approximately 1 km south from Yamato-Asakura Station on the Kintetsu Osaka Line === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === * List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshisaka Nimasu Ikune Jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location=Nara Prefecture, Sakurai City, Oshisaka 871 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 31 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 21 | E/W = E | Enshrined deity=Sukunahikona-no-Kami | Shrine rank=Shikinaisha (Taisha)
Former village shrine | Main hall=None | Sacred object=Miyayama (sacred mountain) | Established=Unknown | Annual festival=October 17 | Map=Japan Nara }} '''Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine''' (おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha (Taisha), and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * Sukunahikona-no-Kami In sources such as the *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō* of Meiji 7 (1874), the enshrined deity is recorded as “Ikune-no-Kami.” However, details regarding this “Ikune-no-Kami” are unknown. === History === === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown, but since the name appears as “Ikune Shrine households” in the *Yamato Province Shōzeichō* of Tenpyō 2 (730), and as “Ikune Shrine, one household, Yamato” in the *Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō* of Daidō 1 (806), it is believed to have been founded earlier than these records. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, it is listed as a Taisha and noted as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals. During the Edo period, it was simply called “Ikune Shrine,” and because there are also sacred rock formations (iwakura), it is believed that rituals were conducted at this site without relocation. In the past, there was also a shrine-temple (jingūji), the Ishii-dera Yakushi Hall, built in Genroku 2 (1689). === Divine rank === * January 27, Ten’an 3 (859): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (as recorded in the *Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) — recorded under the name “Oshisaka Ikune Shrine.” === Precincts === There is no main hall at this shrine. To the north of the worship hall, 21 natural stones known as “Ishigami” are arranged and venerated as the sacred object in the form of an iwakura. === Access === * Approximately 1 km south from Yamato-Asakura Station on the Kintetsu Osaka Line === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === * List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshisaka Nimasu Ikune Jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a Shikinaisha (Taisha), and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * Sukunahikona-no-Kami In sources such as the *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō* of Meiji 7 (1874), the enshrined deity is recorded as “Ikune-no-Kami.” However, details regarding this “Ikune-no-Kami” are unknown. === History === === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown, but since the name appears as “Ikune Shrine households” in the *Yamato Province Shōzeichō* of Tenpyō 2 (730), and as “Ikune Shrine, one household, Yamato” in the *Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō* of Daidō 1 (806), it is believed to have been founded earlier than these records. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, it is listed as a Taisha and noted as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals. During the Edo period, it was simply called “Ikune Shrine,” and because there are also sacred rock formations (iwakura), it is believed that rituals were conducted at this site without relocation. In the past, there was also a shrine-temple (jingūji), the Ishii-dera Yakushi Hall, built in Genroku 2 (1689). === Divine rank === * January 27, Ten’an 3 (859): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (as recorded in the *Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) — recorded under the name “Oshisaka Ikune Shrine.” === Precincts === There is no main hall at this shrine. To the north of the worship hall, 21 natural stones known as “Ishigami” are arranged and venerated as the sacred object in the form of an iwakura. === Access === * Approximately 1 km south from Yamato-Asakura Station on the Kintetsu Osaka Line === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === * List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshisaka Nimasu Ikune Jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

Image={{Image requested|cat=Sakurai City}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location=Nara Prefecture, Sakurai City, Oshisaka 871 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 31 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 21 | E/W = E | Enshrined deity=Sukunahikona-no-Kami | Shrine rank=Shikinaisha (Taisha)
Former village shrine | Main hall=None | Sacred object=Miyayama (sacred mountain) | Established=Unknown | Annual festival=October 17 | Map=Japan Nara }} '''Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine''' (おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha (Taisha), and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * Sukunahikona-no-Kami In sources such as the *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō* of Meiji 7 (1874), the enshrined deity is recorded as “Ikune-no-Kami.” However, details regarding this “Ikune-no-Kami” are unknown. === History === === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown, but since the name appears as “Ikune Shrine households” in the *Yamato Province Shōzeichō* of Tenpyō 2 (730), and as “Ikune Shrine, one household, Yamato” in the *Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō* of Daidō 1 (806), it is believed to have been founded earlier than these records. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, it is listed as a Taisha and noted as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals. During the Edo period, it was simply called “Ikune Shrine,” and because there are also sacred rock formations (iwakura), it is believed that rituals were conducted at this site without relocation. In the past, there was also a shrine-temple (jingūji), the Ishii-dera Yakushi Hall, built in Genroku 2 (1689). === Divine rank === * January 27, Ten’an 3 (859): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (as recorded in the *Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) — recorded under the name “Oshisaka Ikune Shrine.” === Precincts === There is no main hall at this shrine. To the north of the worship hall, 21 natural stones known as “Ishigami” are arranged and venerated as the sacred object in the form of an iwakura. === Access === * Approximately 1 km south from Yamato-Asakura Station on the Kintetsu Osaka Line === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === * List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Oshisaka Nimasu Ikune Jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a Shikinaisha (Taisha), and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * Sukunahikona-no-Kami In sources such as the *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō* of Meiji 7 (1874), the enshrined deity is recorded as “Ikune-no-Kami.” However, details regarding this “Ikune-no-Kami” are unknown. === History === === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown, but since the name appears as “Ikune Shrine households” in the *Yamato Province Shōzeichō* of Tenpyō 2 (730), and as “Ikune Shrine, one household, Yamato” in the *Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō* of Daidō 1 (806), it is believed to have been founded earlier than these records. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, it is listed as a Taisha and noted as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals. During the Edo period, it was simply called “Ikune Shrine,” and because there are also sacred rock formations (iwakura), it is believed that rituals were conducted at this site without relocation. In the past, there was also a shrine-temple (jingūji), the Ishii-dera Yakushi Hall, built in Genroku 2 (1689). === Divine rank === * January 27, Ten’an 3 (859): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (as recorded in the *Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) — recorded under the name “Oshisaka Ikune Shrine.” === Precincts === There is no main hall at this shrine. To the north of the worship hall, 21 natural stones known as “Ishigami” are arranged and venerated as the sacred object in the form of an iwakura. === Access === * Approximately 1 km south from Yamato-Asakura Station on the Kintetsu Osaka Line === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === * List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大おしさかやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

{'text': '奈良県桜井市忍坂871', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:忍坂坐生根神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153274}} == Japanese content == {{Source citation needed|date=2018-3}} {{Shinto shrine | Name=Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine | Image={{Image requested|cat=Sakurai City}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location=Nara Prefecture, Sakurai City, Oshisaka 871 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 31 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 21 | E/W = E | Enshrined deity=Sukunahikona-no-Kami | Shrine rank=Shikinaisha (Taisha)
Former village shrine | Main hall=None | Sacred object=Miyayama (sacred mountain) | Established=Unknown | Annual festival=October 17 | Map=Japan Nara }} '''Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine''' (おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha (Taisha), and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * Sukunahikona-no-Kami In sources such as the *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō* of Meiji 7 (1874), the enshrined deity is recorded as “Ikune-no-Kami.” However, details regarding this “Ikune-no-Kami” are unknown. === History === === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown, but since the name appears as “Ikune Shrine households” in the *Yamato Province Shōzeichō* of Tenpyō 2 (730), and as “Ikune Shrine, one household, Yamato” in the *Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō* of Daidō 1 (806), it is believed to have been founded earlier than these records. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, it is listed as a Taisha and noted as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals. During the Edo period, it was simply called “Ikune Shrine,” and because there are also sacred rock formations (iwakura), it is believed that rituals were conducted at this site without relocation. In the past, there was also a shrine-temple (jingūji), the Ishii-dera Yakushi Hall, built in Genroku 2 (1689). === Divine rank === * January 27, Ten’an 3 (859): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (as recorded in the *Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) — recorded under the name “Oshisaka Ikune Shrine.” === Precincts === There is no main hall at this shrine. To the north of the worship hall, 21 natural stones known as “Ishigami” are arranged and venerated as the sacred object in the form of an iwakura. === Access === * Approximately 1 km south from Yamato-Asakura Station on the Kintetsu Osaka Line === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === * List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location=Nara Prefecture, Sakurai City, Oshisaka 871 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 31 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 21 | E/W = E | Enshrined deity=Sukunahikona-no-Kami | Shrine rank=Shikinaisha (Taisha)
Former village shrine | Main hall=None | Sacred object=Miyayama (sacred mountain) | Established=Unknown | Annual festival=October 17 | Map=Japan Nara }} '''Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine''' (おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha (Taisha), and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * Sukunahikona-no-Kami In sources such as the *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō* of Meiji 7 (1874), the enshrined deity is recorded as “Ikune-no-Kami.” However, details regarding this “Ikune-no-Kami” are unknown. === History === === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown, but since the name appears as “Ikune Shrine households” in the *Yamato Province Shōzeichō* of Tenpyō 2 (730), and as “Ikune Shrine, one household, Yamato” in the *Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō* of Daidō 1 (806), it is believed to have been founded earlier than these records. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, it is listed as a Taisha and noted as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals. During the Edo period, it was simply called “Ikune Shrine,” and because there are also sacred rock formations (iwakura), it is believed that rituals were conducted at this site without relocation. In the past, there was also a shrine-temple (jingūji), the Ishii-dera Yakushi Hall, built in Genroku 2 (1689). === Divine rank === * January 27, Ten’an 3 (859): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (as recorded in the *Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) — recorded under the name “Oshisaka Ikune Shrine.” === Precincts === There is no main hall at this shrine. To the north of the worship hall, 21 natural stones known as “Ishigami” are arranged and venerated as the sacred object in the form of an iwakura. === Access === * Approximately 1 km south from Yamato-Asakura Station on the Kintetsu Osaka Line === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === * List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | Location=Nara Prefecture, Sakurai City, Oshisaka 871 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 31 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 52 | Longitude seconds = 21 | E/W = E | Enshrined deity=Sukunahikona-no-Kami | Shrine rank=Shikinaisha (Taisha)
Former village shrine | Main hall=None | Sacred object=Miyayama (sacred mountain) | Established=Unknown | Annual festival=October 17 | Map=Japan Nara }} '''Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine''' (おしさかにますいくねじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture. It is a Shikinaisha (Taisha), and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === Enshrined deity === * Sukunahikona-no-Kami In sources such as the *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the *Gōsonsha Torishirabechō* of Meiji 7 (1874), the enshrined deity is recorded as “Ikune-no-Kami.” However, details regarding this “Ikune-no-Kami” are unknown. === History === === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown, but since the name appears as “Ikune Shrine households” in the *Yamato Province Shōzeichō* of Tenpyō 2 (730), and as “Ikune Shrine, one household, Yamato” in the *Shinshō Kyakuchokufu-shō* of Daidō 1 (806), it is believed to have been founded earlier than these records. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, it is listed as a Taisha and noted as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals. During the Edo period, it was simply called “Ikune Shrine,” and because there are also sacred rock formations (iwakura), it is believed that rituals were conducted at this site without relocation. In the past, there was also a shrine-temple (jingūji), the Ishii-dera Yakushi Hall, built in Genroku 2 (1689). === Divine rank === * January 27, Ten’an 3 (859): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (as recorded in the *Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) — recorded under the name “Oshisaka Ikune Shrine.” === Precincts === There is no main hall at this shrine. To the north of the worship hall, 21 natural stones known as “Ishigami” are arranged and venerated as the sacred object in the form of an iwakura. === Access === * Approximately 1 km south from Yamato-Asakura Station on the Kintetsu Osaka Line === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === * List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province === External links === * [https://sakurai-kankou.jimdo.com/%E5%90%8D%E6%89%80%E6%97%A7%E8%B7%A1/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E8%A1%97%E9%81%93/%E5%BF%8D%E5%9D%82%E5%9D%90%E7%94%9F%E6%A0%B9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Oshisaka-nimasu Ikune Shrine] – Sakurai City Tourism Association {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Oshisaka Nimai Sukune Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakurai]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]
